name: Lorenz, Perry
resolved_employer: Self-employed downtown Austin real estate developer (Perry Lorenz / co-developer of The Independent, Saltillo Lofts, BartonPlace, Este, Spring, The Pedernales)
evidence: Perry Lorenz is a well-known 50-year Austin real estate developer active since 1977, principal on 1,000+ downtown/East Austin condo units and past chair of the city's Downtown and Design Commissions. Prolific Democratic donor (FEC lean 1.0, 251 gifts); gave to county commissioners Travillion and Howard, consistent with a civically-engaged developer.

name: Kumar, Sam
evidence: Only confirmable fact is a ~$2,500 donation to a Travis County Democratic commissioners candidate (2022). No zip, occupation, employer, or FEC history in the record; 'Sam Kumar' is a common name and the specific individual cannot be corroborated. Not classified.

evidence: Darryl Bernard Carter is a Houston business-litigation attorney; his firm's address (4828 Loop Central Dr, Ste 600, Houston, TX 77081) exactly matches the donor's 77081 location. Texas Bar member, UT Law grad. Democratic-leaning donor (FEC lean 1.0); gave to commissioner Travillion.

name: Graham, Charles

name: Bullock, Dan
resolved_employer: Retired banking/financial-management executive and Austin civic leader; founder of The Mosey Project (retired)
evidence: Dan Bullock of Austin (zip 78763) is a 30+ year banking/financial-management executive and prominent civic leader — former president of Leadership Austin, Texas Lyceum, Umlauf Sculpture Garden, Ronald McDonald House, Zach Theatre — and founder of The Mosey Project. name: Scanlan, John
resolved_employer: John McAllen Scanlan — retired Austin attorney; founder/director of the UT legal clinic and former City of West Lake Hills attorney (retired)
evidence: John McAllen Scanlan, retired Austin lawyer, lists his practice address at Wild Basin Ledge, West Lake Hills, TX 78746-2707 — an exact match to the donor's location. Founded the UT legal clinic, served as City of West Lake Hills attorney, and sat on the LCRA board and a Lake Travis/Lake Austin water-quality committee.
